text: In this video, we present a robotic transcendental excision of a rectal leiomyoma with DaVinci XI system. This was a fifty-eight years old lady diagnosed with a one centimeter tumoral lesion located at the upper rectum. Preoperative rectal endosynography revealed a submucosal lesion suggesting a gastrointestinal

The patient is positioned in a prone jackknife position for the procedure. A gel point pad is used as a single port trocar, fixed into the anal canal. Three 8mm robotic ports are placed, and the rectum is insufflated with CO₂. Robotic arms are docked from the left side of the patient.

The surgeon marks the resection margin using robotic superficial tip cautery. The mucosa over the rectal lesion is gently grasped with a Maryland bipolar forceps, and the lesion is excised completely using the superficial tip cautery.
The surgeon closes the defect on the rectal wall using a continuous three OV lock suture after confirming negative surgical margins from the frozen section of the excised lesion.
The blood loss was three milliliters after procedure and the patient stayed two days in the hospital without any complication.
